headlines.title: In pictures: Stars and fashion from the red carpet
headlines.description: Hollywood's biggest stars turn on the style as they walk the red carpet for the glittering ceremony.

headlines.title: How US groups are driving a new generation of anti-abortion activism in the UK
headlines.description: The killing of Charlie Kirk galvanised a transatlantic campaign against abortion. But will it succeed in shifting Britain's pro-choice consensus?

headlines.title: Starmer to set out support plan for heating oil costs
headlines.description: Households have faced a sharp rise in the cost of heating oil since the outbreak of the US-Israeli war with Iran.

headlines.title: Two people die, including university student, in Kent meningitis outbreak
headlines.description: Eleven people in the Canterbury area are also seriously ill in hospital, the BBC understands.

headlines.title: TikTok and Meta risked safety to win algorithm arms race, whistleblowers say
headlines.description: Companies allowed more harmful content on user’s feeds, knowing their algorithms ran on outrage, BBC hears.

headlines.title: Historic firsts, emotional speeches, and a tie: Watch the top Oscars moments
headlines.description: One Battle After Another dominated the night with six awards, while Sinners and Frankenstein weren't far behind.

headlines.title: Winners list in full
headlines.description: Find out which films and stars have won the famous golden statuettes at the ceremony in Los Angeles.

headlines.title: Here are all the moments you didn't see on TV
headlines.description: Inside the winners room and other insights from behind the scenes.

headlines.title: Best animated film winner Kpop Demon Hunters captures hearts world over
headlines.description: We find out how the award-winning, demon-busting crooners have cast a spell world over.

headlines.title: Six talking points from final round of Six Nations
headlines.description: England's indiscipline, "cheat code" Louis Bielle-Biarrey, Irish and Scottish positives, and Welsh confidence returns - talking points from the final round.

headlines.title: We didn't view Dad's body after being told it had changed so much
headlines.description: Ronald Parkin's case prompts calls from his family for the government to regulate the funeral sector.

headlines.title: Race on to establish globally recognised 'AI-free' logo
headlines.description: The backlash to the growing use of the tech has led to an explosion in attempts to come up with 'AI-Free' logo that could be used globally.

headlines.title: How passenger planes keep flying during a war
headlines.description: Airspace over Iran and the Gulf remains severely restricted following missile strikes, putting additional pressure on airlines.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Help' for energy bills and PM 'resists' Trump warships call
headlines.description: The impact in the UK of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East features on Monday's front pages.

headlines.title: Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
headlines.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

headlines.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
headlines.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

headlines.title: 'A new norm': BBC visits Doha market starting to fill up again two weeks into Iran war
headlines.description: The BBC's International News correspondent, Barbara Plett Usher, visits Doha's Souq Waqif market as strikes in the region continue.

headlines.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
headlines.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

headlines.title: Firms to be paid to hire unemployed young people
headlines.description: Payments of £3,000 for each 18-24 year old given a job are among proposals to tackle youth unemployment being announced later.

headlines.title: Starmer warned over assisted dying bill by more than 100 Labour MPs
headlines.description: More than 100 Labour MPs warn that failing to pass the legislation would undermine trust in politics.

headlines.title: People turn to private health care to beat NHS waits, says watchdog
headlines.description: The patient watchdog warns of two-tier service as polling shows numbers paying for care is on the rise.

headlines.title: Travelodge changes policy after attacker given room key
headlines.description: The woman was attacked by Kyran Smith, who was given a key to her hotel room by staff.

headlines.title: One-off £2,000 grant gives care leavers head start, study finds
headlines.description: Participants were less likely to become homeless or spend time in hospital or prison, researchers say.

headlines.title: Police investigate 'death to the IDF' chants led by Bobby Vylan at rally
headlines.description: Around 1,000 officers managed protesters and counter-protesters on opposite sides of the River Thames.

headlines.title: Mother charged with murder of 18-day-old baby
headlines.description: Zahira Byjaouane, 43, has been charged after her baby died falling from a height in central London.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Will Starmer Help Trump In The War?
headlines.description: Trump wants help to secure the Strait of Hormuz but will the UK join him?

headlines.title: Is Cuba Trump’s next target after Iran?
headlines.description: The US president threatens Cuba with a “friendly takeover”

headlines.title: We have to wake up - Szoboszlai warning sums up Liverpool plight
headlines.description: Liverpool have been here far too often this season. Another late, late goal costing them points as they drew 1-1 with struggling Tottenham.

headlines.title: Six talking points from final round of Six Nations
headlines.description: England's indiscipline, "cheat code" Louis Bielle-Biarrey, Irish and Scottish positives, and Welsh confidence returns - talking points from the final round.

headlines.title: Young snatches Players title from Fitzpatrick
headlines.description: American Cameron Young finishes birdie-par to beat England's Matt Fitzpatrick by one shot at TPC Sawgrass in the PGA Tour's flagship tournament.

headlines.title: Sinner matches Federer & Djokovic with Indian Wells win
headlines.description: Jannik Sinner said it "means a lot to me" after he becomes the youngest man to complete the set of hard-court trophies with victory over Daniil Medvedev in the Indian Wells final.

headlines.title: 'Best racing ever' or 'a joke'? The row over the new F1
headlines.description: Have F1's new rules produced a "battery world championship" or "the best racing ever"? Lewis Hamilton, Max Verstappen and Fernando Alonso sum up the paradox at the heart of the sport.

headlines.title: New puppy, getting engaged and a title - Sabalenka triumphs at Indian Wells
headlines.description: Getting a puppy, getting engaged, saving championship point and winning a long-awaited Indian Wells title - for Aryna Sabalenka it has been a week she will remember "for the rest of my life".
